metadata.name: Ravenous — Tyrranax Rex enters with X counters and draws when X >= 5
metadata.description: Tests both effects of the Ravenous keyword (CR 702.156a) on Tyrranax Rex. P1 casts Tyrranax Rex with X=5, paying {5}{G}{G}{G}{G}. Ravenous is a replacement effect that causes the permanent to enter with X +1/+1 counters (so Tyrranax Rex enters as a 13/13 instead of 8/8). Because X is 5 or more, a triggered ability also fires: 'When this permanent enters, if X is 5 or more, draw a card.' The trigger resolves and p1 draws a card, increasing hand count from 0 to 1.
metadata.generation_notes: Tyrranax Rex card definition (crates/engine/src/cards/defs/tyrranax_rex.rs): mana_cost green:4 (represents {X}{G}{G}{G}{G}); base P/T 8/8; abilities: Trample, Ward(4), Ravenous. CR 702.156a: Ravenous represents both a replacement effect (enters with X +1/+1 counters) and a triggered ability (draw a card if X >= 5). With X=5, p1 needs 5 colorless + 4 green = 9 total mana. The mana pool is pre-loaded with 5 colorless + 4 green. Tyrranax Rex enters with 5 +1/+1 counters making it a 13/13. The draw trigger fires immediately after it enters; the stack_resolve for Tyrranax Rex leaves a trigger on the stack. A card must be in p1's library for the draw to succeed — one Forest is placed in library. After the trigger resolves p1 has 1 card in hand (the drawn Forest). Ward {4} is irrelevant here — nothing targets Tyrranax Rex in this script. The script uses x_value: 5 per CR 107.3m and the script_schema.rs x_value field (line 342).

script.step_note: p1 casts Tyrranax Rex with X=5; it resolves entering with 5 +1/+1 counters; draw trigger fires and p1 draws a card

actions.description: Initial: Tyrranax Rex in p1's hand, 1 card in library, stack empty

actions.note: CR 601.2: p1 casts Tyrranax Rex with X=5, paying {5}{G}{G}{G}{G}. CR 107.3m: X is chosen as 5 at cast time. Tyrranax Rex moves from hand to the stack.

actions.description: After casting: Tyrranax Rex on stack, hand empty

actions.note: CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — Tyrranax Rex resolves.

resolution.note: CR 608.2: Tyrranax Rex resolves and enters the battlefield. CR 702.156a: Ravenous replacement effect applies — it enters with 5 +1/+1 counters (X=5) instead of 0, making it effectively a 13/13. Because X >= 5, the Ravenous draw trigger is placed on the stack.

actions.note: CR 702.156a: Ravenous — permanent enters with X +1/+1 counters (replacement effect, not a trigger). The 'if X is 5 or more, draw a card' triggered ability fires and goes on the stack (CR 603.2).

actions.description: After Tyrranax Rex enters: it is on battlefield with 5 +1/+1 counters; draw trigger is on stack
actions.note: CR 702.156a: The Ravenous draw trigger ('if X is 5 or more, draw a card') is on the stack awaiting resolution. Tyrranax Rex is on the battlefield with 5 +1/+1 counters.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — Ravenous draw trigger resolves.

resolution.note: CR 702.156a: Ravenous triggered ability resolves — p1 draws 1 card (the Forest from library). Hand count increases from 0 to 1.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Ravenous draw trigger resolves; p1 draws a card.

actions.description: Final: Tyrranax Rex on battlefield with 5 counters, p1 drew 1 card, stack empty
actions.note: CR 702.156a: Both Ravenous effects resolved — Tyrranax Rex entered with 5 +1/+1 counters (replacement effect) and p1 drew 1 card (triggered ability, X >= 5). Stack is empty.
